questions.question: On which date did the Constitution of India come into force?
The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a:
From whom does the Constitution of India derive its authority?
The term "Sovereign" in the Preamble signifies that India is:
options: A member of the British Commonwealth

questions.question: How many Articles and Schedules were originally contained in the Constitution of India?
Which case established that federal features form part of the "basic structure" of the Indian Constitution?
options: A.K. Gopalan v. State of Madras
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
questions.answer: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an essential feature of a federal system according to the text?
In India, residuary powers of legislation are vested in:
The Indian Constitution is often described as:
Unlike the American federal system, India has:
Under Article 12, the definition of "State" includes:
options: Only the Government and Parliament of India
Government, Parliament, State Legislatures, and all local or other authorities
questions.answer: Government, Parliament, State Legislatures, and all local or other authorities

questions.question: According to the Supreme Court in the BCCI case (Zee Telefilms Ltd. v. Union of India), the BCCI is:
options: Not a State because it is not financially or functionally dominated by the Government
questions.answer: Not a State because it is not financially or functionally dominated by the Government

questions.question: Article 13(1) declares that pre-Constitutional laws are void to the extent of their inconsistency with:
The "Doctrine of Severability" means:
options: The entire law is void if one part is unconstitutional
Only the inconsistent part of the law is void, if it can be separated from the valid part
The President can sever any law
questions.answer: Only the inconsistent part of the law is void, if it can be separated from the valid part

questions.question: The "Doctrine of Eclipse" applies primarily to:
options: Pre-Constitutional laws that become inconsistent with Fundamental Rights
questions.answer: Pre-Constitutional laws that become inconsistent with Fundamental Rights

questions.question: Article 14 guarantees equality before the law to:
options: All persons, including juristic persons like corporations
questions.answer: All persons, including juristic persons like corporations

questions.question: To be valid, a legislative classification under Article 14 must satisfy:
options: The twin test of Intelligible Differentia and Rational Nexus
The approval of the Supreme Court only
questions.answer: The twin test of Intelligible Differentia and Rational Nexus

questions.question: Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
Article 15(3) allows the State to make special provisions for:
The 103rd Amendment Act, 2019, introduced reservations for:
Article 16(1) guarantees equality of opportunity in:
According to the text, the ceiling for reservation of vacancies in a year (excluding carry-forward) is generally:
How many fundamental freedoms are currently guaranteed under Article 19(1)?
Which of the following is NOT a freedom listed under Article 19?
options: Freedom to assemble peaceably without arms
Freedom to form associations or unions

questions.question: Restrictions on the freedoms under Article 19 must be:
The "Right to Know" and "Freedom of the Press" are derived from which Article?
Article 20(2) provides protection against "Double Jeopardy," which means:
options: No person can be arrested without a warrant
No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once
No person can be a witness against himself
No person can be fined twice for a civil wrong
questions.answer: No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once

questions.question: Protection against self-incrimination is provided under:
Article 21 states that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to:
Which case expanded Article 21 to include the "Right to travel abroad"?
options: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
R.K. Garg v. Union of India
questions.answer: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India

questions.question: The Right to Education (Article 21A) provides free and compulsory education to children of age:
Prohibition of "Begar" and forced labour is guaranteed by:
Article 24 prohibits the employment of children below the age of ____ in factories or mines.
Which Article is described as the "Heart and Soul" of the Constitution?
Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) are:
options: Non-justiciable but fundamental in the governance of the country
Only applicable to the Union Government
questions.answer: Non-justiciable but fundamental in the governance of the country

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to organize Village Panchayats?
The State shall endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code under:
Fundamental Duties (Article 51A) were added by which Amendment?
The President's power to promulgate Ordinances is found in:
An Ordinance promulgated by the President must be laid before Parliament and ceases to operate after ____ from the re-assembly of Parliament.
The Governor's power to make Ordinances is provided under:
Which Schedule contains the Union, State, and Concurrent Lists?
In case of conflict between a Central law and a State law on a subject in the Concurrent List:
options: The Supreme Court decides which prevails

questions.question: Pith and Substance is a rule used for:
options: Resolving conflicts between legislative entries in different lists
Determining the age of a judge
questions.answer: Resolving conflicts between legislative entries in different lists

questions.question: Parliament can legislate on a matter in the State List in the National Interest if the ____ passes a resolution.
Which writ is issued to produce a person who is wrongfully detained?
The writ of "Mandamus" literally means:
A writ of "Quo Warranto" is used to:
options: Compel a public official to do their duty
Enquire into the legality of a claim to a public office
Stop a lower court from exceeding its jurisdiction
questions.answer: Enquire into the legality of a claim to a public office

questions.question: Article 226 gives writ jurisdiction to:
The "Doctrine of Basic Structure" limits the power of Parliament to:
